sections.paragraph:
an unused root, Arabic لَاحَ to shine, to be bright (comp. λευκός, γλαυκός; λεύσσω, γλαύσσω); hence to be polished, smooth. Hence is-

לוח

m. pl. לוּחֹות a table, tablet (Syr. ܠܘܽܚܐܳ; Arab. لُوْحُ; Ethiopic ለውህ፡: id.)-(a) of stone, on which anything was carved, or inscribed; לוּחֹת הַבְּרִית Deu 9:9 לֻחֹת הָעֵדוּת Exo 31:18, the tables of covenant, of law.-(b) of wood, 1Ki 7:36 of the leaf of a door, Son 8:9 Dual לֻחֹתַיִם the deck of a ship, which seems to have been double, Eze 27:5.-(c) trop. Pro 3:3, “write upon the table of thy heart;” compare Jer 17:1 2Co 3:3 and δέλτοι φρενῶν, Æsch. Whence-
